title: Neobouteloua lophostachya
taxonomy: Neobouteloua lophostachya is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Stipeae. The genus Neobouteloua is part of the larger group of grasses known as the bromoid grasses. These grasses are characterized by their narrow leaves and spikelets, which are the main reproductive structures of the plant. Neobouteloua lophostachya is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.

intro: Neobouteloua lophostachya is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neobouteloua lophostachya include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
